raw_text: Conversational capture reads assistant and user turns from the session transcript and ingests the persisted sentences.
note: transcript kind; raw_meta null so derive() takes the agent_inferred path, replay-stable under raw_meta=None.

raw_text: The ingest-transcript subcommand walks session JSONL files and appends one ingest_log row per persisted sentence.
note: transcript kind; factual default, no requirement/preference/correction keywords.

raw_text: Transcript rows carry a call_site of transcript_ingest so the corroboration source is distinguishable from filesystem scanning.
note: transcript kind; describes the corroboration-source tag.

raw_text: Assistant-role transcript lines are excluded from belief creation by the speaker-attribution gate.
note: transcript kind; design statement about the #785 gate.

raw_text: Idempotency on a transcript turn resolves the same sentence to the same belief id via a content-hash lookup.
The derivation worker runs once at end of turn and stamps the log rows with the derived belief ids.
note: transcript kind; describes the per-batch worker invocation.

raw_text: Sub-floor clauses in a turn attach as anchor_text on a DERIVED_FROM edge between the surrounding full-length beliefs.
note: transcript kind; describes the #809 sub-floor filter.

raw_text: Session-scoped retrieval skips beliefs whose session_id column is null so legacy rows produce no false positives.
Conversational capture defaults to the fact retention class, the same default the filesystem scanner uses.
note: transcript kind; documents the retention default preserved by the rename.

raw_text: Passive capture is a distinct ingest source from project-file scanning even though both feed the deterministic classifier.
note: transcript kind; the provenance distinction this source kind exists to make.
